figures.caption: Table 2: Main performance comparison. The best results are highlighted in bold and the second best are underlined.
Figure 12: Visualization of prediction performance with varying context lengths (Top-𝐾 ). The plots display the concatenated input sequences and resulting predictions for 𝐾 ∈ {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}.
Figure 11: Comparison ofweighting strategies: Uniform (Unweighted), Temporal Weight Only, and our proposed Variable + Temporal strategy.
Figure 9: Visualization of covariate ablation studies.We compare prediction trajectories under four settings: (1) No Covariates, (2) Only IP, (3) Only N2, and (4) Full Covariates.

Figure 10: Impact of Knowledge Base (KB) Scope on retrieval and prediction. From top to bottom: Scope 1 (Single Plane), Scope 2 (B777, same aircraft type), and Scope 3 (B777 + A320, cross-Type mixed aircraft).
Figure 2: Overall RAG Pipeline. Tail numbers are masked for privacy.
Table 6: Ablation on weighting schemes and context length (𝑘) on B777. “𝑊 point” for PointWeighting and “𝑊 cov” for Covariate Weighting.
Table 7: Predictive maintenance records from the CSA deployment (Starting Nov. 2025). Online visualizations are presented in Appendix A.7. Tail numbers are masked for privacy.
Figure 13: The MSE of the B-2**7 PRSOV L in time order. PRSOV L fault notified and detected with our method on plane B-2**7.
Figure 5: The agentic context augment process. The top-1 sample is used as an agent to self-calibrate the optimal number of context fragments (𝑘∗). Tail numbers are masked for privacy.
